1	package bead
2	
3	import (
4		"strings"
5		"testing"
6	
7		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
8		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
9	)
10	
11	// TestSchemaAcceptsOperatorPromptIssueType validates that a JSON record with
12	// issue_type=operator-prompt and status=proposed passes the bead-record JSON
13	// schema (Story 15 §Implementation #1: schema validator must accept the new
14	// issueType and the new proposed status).
15	func TestSchemaAcceptsOperatorPromptIssueType(t *testing.T) {
16		schema := compileBeadSchema(t)
17	
18		doc := `{"id":"ddx-op-1","title":"do a thing","status":"proposed","priority":2,"issue_type":"operator-prompt","created_at":"2026-05-02T00:00:00Z","updated_at":"2026-05-02T00:00:00Z"}`
19		require.NoError(t, validateJSONAgainstSchema(t, schema, doc))
20	
21		cancelled := `{"id":"ddx-op-2","title":"cancel me","status":"cancelled","priority":2,"issue_type":"operator-prompt","created_at":"2026-05-02T00:00:00Z","updated_at":"2026-05-02T00:00:00Z"}`
22		require.NoError(t, validateJSONAgainstSchema(t, schema, cancelled))
23	}
24	
25	// TestStoreAcceptsOperatorPromptBead exercises the validator on the live
26	// store path: an operator-prompt bead in proposed status must persist and
27	// re-load round-trip clean.
28	func TestStoreAcceptsOperatorPromptBead(t *testing.T) {
29		store := newTestStore(t)
30		b := NewOperatorPromptBead("create a P2 bead titled 'demo'\nbody line two", 2)
31		require.Equal(t, IssueTypeOperatorPrompt, b.IssueType)
32		require.Equal(t, StatusProposed, b.Status)
33		require.NoError(t, store.Create(b))
34	
35		got, err := store.Get(b.ID)
36		require.NoError(t, err)
37		assert.Equal(t, IssueTypeOperatorPrompt, got.IssueType)
38		assert.Equal(t, StatusProposed, got.Status)
39		assert.Contains(t, got.Labels, OperatorPromptLabelKind)
40		assert.Contains(t, got.Labels, OperatorPromptLabelSource)
41		assert.Equal(t, OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance, got.Acceptance)
42		assert.Equal(t, "create a P2 bead titled 'demo'", got.Title)
43		assert.True(t, strings.Contains(got.Description, "body line two"))
44	}
45	
46	// TestNewOperatorPromptBeadDefaults covers tier clamping and the
47	// empty-prompt fallback so operators cannot smuggle in nonsense priorities
48	// or zero-length titles.
49	func TestNewOperatorPromptBeadDefaults(t *testing.T) {
50		clamped := NewOperatorPromptBead("p", 99)
51		assert.Equal(t, MaxPriority, clamped.Priority)
52	
53		negative := NewOperatorPromptBead("p", -3)
54		assert.Equal(t, MinPriority, negative.Priority)
55	
56		empty := NewOperatorPromptBead("   \n   ", 2)
57		assert.Equal(t, "(empty operator prompt)", empty.Title)
58	}
59	
60	// TestStatusTransitionsForProposed locks down the documented state machine:
61	// proposed → open and proposed → cancelled are allowed; everything else
62	// out of proposed is rejected. Callers (UI approval flow) rely on this
63	// envelope to decide which buttons to enable.
64	func TestStatusTransitionsForProposed(t *testing.T) {
65		cases := []struct {
66			from, to string
67			want     bool
68		}{
69			{StatusProposed, StatusOpen, true},
70			{StatusProposed, StatusCancelled, true},
71			{StatusProposed, StatusInProgress, false},
72			{StatusProposed, StatusClosed, false},
73			{StatusProposed, StatusBlocked, false},
74			{StatusProposed, StatusProposed, false},
75			{StatusOpen, StatusInProgress, true},
76			{StatusInProgress, StatusClosed, true},
77			{StatusClosed, StatusOpen, true},
78			{StatusCancelled, StatusOpen, true},
79			{"", StatusOpen, false},
80			{StatusOpen, "", false},
81			{"bogus", StatusOpen, false},
82		}
83		for _, tc := range cases {
84			t.Run(tc.from+"->"+tc.to, func(t *testing.T) {
85				assert.Equal(t, tc.want, IsValidStatusTransition(tc.from, tc.to))
86			})
87		}
88	}
89	
90	// TestOperatorPromptMutationGuardMatrix covers the 2x2 actor/target matrix
91	// from Story 15 §Additional security controls: only an operator-prompt
92	// actor mutating an operator-prompt target is denied.
93	func TestOperatorPromptMutationGuardMatrix(t *testing.T) {
94		cases := []struct {
95			name        string
96			actor       string
97			target      string
98			expectAllow bool
99		}{
100			{"no actor allows everything", "", IssueTypeOperatorPrompt, true},
101			{"task actor → operator-prompt target allowed", "task", IssueTypeOperatorPrompt, true},
102			{"operator-prompt actor → task target allowed", IssueTypeOperatorPrompt, "task", true},
103			{"operator-prompt actor → empty target allowed", IssueTypeOperatorPrompt, "", true},
104			{"operator-prompt actor → operator-prompt target denied", IssueTypeOperatorPrompt, IssueTypeOperatorPrompt, false},
105		}
106		for _, tc := range cases {
107			t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
108				err := OperatorPromptMutationGuard(tc.actor, tc.target)
109				if tc.expectAllow {
110					assert.NoError(t, err)
111					return
112				}
113				require.Error(t, err)
114				assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "operator-prompt")
115			})
116		}
117	}
118	
119	// TestStoreCreateRejectsOperatorPromptSelfMutation verifies the no-self-mutation
120	// rule is enforced at bead-create time when the actor's issue_type is exposed
121	// via DDX_ACTOR_ISSUE_TYPE (set by the execute-bead harness). A task actor must
122	// be allowed to create operator-prompt beads; only operator-prompt → operator-prompt
123	// is denied.
124	func TestStoreCreateRejectsOperatorPromptSelfMutation(t *testing.T) {
125		store := newTestStore(t)
126	
127		t.Setenv("DDX_ACTOR_ISSUE_TYPE", IssueTypeOperatorPrompt)
128		denied := NewOperatorPromptBead("nested prompt", 2)
129		err := store.Create(denied)
130		require.Error(t, err)
131		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "operator-prompt")
132	
133		t.Setenv("DDX_ACTOR_ISSUE_TYPE", "task")
134		allowed := NewOperatorPromptBead("first prompt", 2)
135		require.NoError(t, store.Create(allowed))
136	
137		t.Setenv("DDX_ACTOR_ISSUE_TYPE", "")
138		human := NewOperatorPromptBead("human-submitted prompt", 2)
139		require.NoError(t, store.Create(human))
140	}
141	
142	// TestStoreRejectsInvalidStatus protects the validateBead enum boundary:
143	// arbitrary status strings must still be rejected even though the enum
144	// grew to include `proposed` and `cancelled`.
145	func TestStoreRejectsInvalidStatus(t *testing.T) {
146		store := newTestStore(t)
147		b := &Bead{Title: "bad status", IssueType: "task", Priority: 2, Status: "weird"}
148		err := store.Create(b)
149		require.Error(t, err)
150		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "invalid status")
151	}
152

1	package bead
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strings"
6		"time"
7	)
8	
9	// Bead represents a portable work item with metadata.
10	// The schema matches bd/br JSONL format for interchange compatibility.
11	// Unknown fields from external sources are preserved in Extra.
12	type Bead struct {
13		ID        string    `json:"id"`
14		Title     string    `json:"title"`
15		Status    string    `json:"status"`
16		Priority  int       `json:"priority"`
17		IssueType string    `json:"issue_type"`
18		Owner     string    `json:"owner,omitempty"`
19		CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
20		CreatedBy string    `json:"created_by,omitempty"`
21		UpdatedAt time.Time `json:"updated_at"`
22	
23		// Optional fields (bd-compatible)
24		Labels      []string `json:"labels,omitempty"`
25		Parent      string   `json:"parent,omitempty"`
26		Description string   `json:"description,omitempty"`
27		Acceptance  string   `json:"acceptance,omitempty"`
28		Notes       string   `json:"notes,omitempty"`
29	
30		// Dependencies use bd-compatible format
31		Dependencies []Dependency `json:"dependencies,omitempty"`
32	
33		// Extra holds unknown fields for round-trip preservation.
34		// Workflow-specific fields (e.g. HELIX spec-id, execution-eligible)
35		// are stored here and written back on save.
36		Extra map[string]any `json:"-"`
37	}
38	
39	// Dependency represents a link between two beads (bd-compatible format).
40	type Dependency struct {
41		IssueID     string `json:"issue_id"`
42		DependsOnID string `json:"depends_on_id"`
43		Type        string `json:"type"` // "blocks", "related", etc.
44		CreatedAt   string `json:"created_at,omitempty"`
45		CreatedBy   string `json:"created_by,omitempty"`
46		Metadata    string `json:"metadata,omitempty"`
47	}
48	
49	// BeadEvent records append-only execution evidence.
50	type BeadEvent struct {
51		Kind      string    `json:"kind"`
52		Summary   string    `json:"summary,omitempty"`
53		Body      string    `json:"body,omitempty"`
54		Actor     string    `json:"actor,omitempty"`
55		CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
56		Source    string    `json:"source,omitempty"`
57	}
58	
59	// Status constants
60	const (
61		StatusOpen       = "open"
62		StatusInProgress = "in_progress"
63		StatusClosed     = "closed"
64		// StatusBlocked marks a bead that is not eligible for agent dispatch —
65		// either decomposed into child beads by the triage gate or parked for
66		// human intervention after a triage-overflow event. Blocked beads are
67		// excluded from ReadyExecution and will not be auto-dispatched.
68		StatusBlocked = "blocked"
69		// StatusProposed marks an operator-prompt bead awaiting approval. It is
70		// excluded from execute-loop drain until transitioned to open via the
71		// approval flow, or to cancelled when the operator declines it.
72		StatusProposed = "proposed"
73		// StatusCancelled marks a bead that was rejected before any execution
74		// took place (e.g. an operator-prompt bead the user chose not to run).
75		StatusCancelled = "cancelled"
76	)
77	
78	// IssueType constants
79	const (
80		// IssueTypeOperatorPrompt is the bead type used for operator-submitted
81		// prompts that the execute-loop runs as instructions.
82		IssueTypeOperatorPrompt = "operator-prompt"
83	)
84	
85	// Default labels and other defaults for operator-prompt beads.
86	const (
87		OperatorPromptLabelKind   = "kind:operator-prompt"
88		OperatorPromptLabelSource = "source:web-ui"
89		// OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance is the auto-AC stub used when an
90		// operator submits a prompt without explicit acceptance criteria. The
91		// structural AC verifier is skipped for operator-prompt beads, so this
92		// stub stands as a human-readable contract only.
93		OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance = "Agent must produce a diff or no_changes rationale; the prompt body is the contract."
94	)
95	
96	// IsValidStatusTransition reports whether a bead may move from `from` to `to`
97	// under the documented state machine. Self-transitions (from == to) and
98	// transitions to/from the empty string (used by Create defaults) are rejected;
99	// callers that want to seed a fresh status should set b.Status directly before
100	// validateBead runs.
101	//
102	// Allowed transitions:
103	//
104	//	open        → in_progress, closed, blocked
105	//	in_progress → open, closed, blocked
106	//	blocked     → open, closed
107	//	proposed    → open, cancelled
108	//	closed      → open                (reopen)
109	//	cancelled   → open                (reopen)
110	func IsValidStatusTransition(from, to string) bool {
111		if from == "" || to == "" || from == to {
112			return false
113		}
114		allowed := map[string]map[string]bool{
115			StatusOpen:       {StatusInProgress: true, StatusClosed: true, StatusBlocked: true},
116			StatusInProgress: {StatusOpen: true, StatusClosed: true, StatusBlocked: true},
117			StatusBlocked:    {StatusOpen: true, StatusClosed: true},
118			StatusProposed:   {StatusOpen: true, StatusCancelled: true},
119			StatusClosed:     {StatusOpen: true},
120			StatusCancelled:  {StatusOpen: true},
121		}
122		if next, ok := allowed[from]; ok {
123			return next[to]
124		}
125		return false
126	}
127	
128	// OperatorPromptMutationGuard enforces the no-self-mutation rule from
129	// Story 15: an operator-prompt bead's execution may not create, edit, or
130	// close another operator-prompt bead. The guard returns nil when the
131	// mutation is allowed and a non-nil error when it must be rejected.
132	//
133	// actorIssueType is the issue_type of the bead currently being executed
134	// (empty when no operator-prompt context is active). targetIssueType is
135	// the issue_type of the bead about to be mutated.
136	//
137	// Allow/deny matrix:
138	//
139	//	actor=""               , target=*                → allow
140	//	actor="task"           , target=*                → allow
141	//	actor="operator-prompt", target!="operator-prompt" → allow
142	//	actor="operator-prompt", target=="operator-prompt" → deny
143	func OperatorPromptMutationGuard(actorIssueType, targetIssueType string) error {
144		if actorIssueType != IssueTypeOperatorPrompt {
145			return nil
146		}
147		if targetIssueType != IssueTypeOperatorPrompt {
148			return nil
149		}
150		return fmt.Errorf("bead: operator-prompt bead may not mutate another operator-prompt bead")
151	}
152	
153	// NewOperatorPromptBead constructs a fresh operator-prompt bead from a raw
154	// prompt string, applying the Story 15 template defaults: title is the first
155	// non-empty line of the prompt, full prompt body is preserved verbatim in the
156	// description, default labels are kind:operator-prompt + source:web-ui, the
157	// status starts in `proposed` (approval flow), priority defaults to the
158	// caller-supplied tier (clamped to MinPriority..MaxPriority), and the
159	// acceptance field carries the auto-AC stub.
160	//
161	// The returned bead is not persisted; callers feed it to Store.Create which
162	// will assign the ID and CreatedAt/UpdatedAt timestamps.
163	func NewOperatorPromptBead(prompt string, defaultTier int) *Bead {
164		body := strings.TrimSpace(prompt)
165		title := body
166		if i := strings.IndexByte(body, '\n'); i >= 0 {
167			title = strings.TrimSpace(body[:i])
168		}
169		if title == "" {
170			title = "(empty operator prompt)"
171		}
172		tier := defaultTier
173		if tier < MinPriority {
174			tier = MinPriority
175		}
176		if tier > MaxPriority {
177			tier = MaxPriority
178		}
179		return &Bead{
180			Title:       title,
181			IssueType:   IssueTypeOperatorPrompt,
182			Status:      StatusProposed,
183			Priority:    tier,
184			Labels:      []string{OperatorPromptLabelKind, OperatorPromptLabelSource},
185			Description: body,
186			Acceptance:  OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance,
187		}
188	}
189	
190	// Default values
191	const (
192		DefaultType     = "task"
193		DefaultStatus   = StatusOpen
194		DefaultPriority = 2
195		DefaultPrefix   = "bx" // used only when repo name detection fails
196		MinPriority     = 0
197		MaxPriority     = 4
198	)
199	
200	// StatusCounts holds aggregate counts for a bead store.
201	type StatusCounts struct {
202		Open    int `json:"open"`
203		Closed  int `json:"closed"`
204		Blocked int `json:"blocked"`
205		Ready   int `json:"ready"`
206		Total   int `json:"total"`
207	}
208	
209	// Blocker kinds surfaced through BlockedAll. These strings are part of the
210	// external DDx contract (HELIX reads them to decide how to handle a blocker).
211	const (
212		BlockerKindDependency    = "dependency"
213		BlockerKindRetryCooldown = "retry-cooldown"
214	)
215	
216	// Blocker describes why an open bead is currently not runnable. Either
217	// unclosed dependencies exist, or an execute-loop cooldown has parked the
218	// bead until NextEligibleAt.
219	type Blocker struct {
220		Kind           string   `json:"kind"`
221		NextEligibleAt string   `json:"next_eligible_at,omitempty"`
222		UnclosedDepIDs []string `json:"unclosed_dep_ids,omitempty"`
223		LastStatus     string   `json:"last_status,omitempty"`
224		LastDetail     string   `json:"last_detail,omitempty"`
225	}
226	
227	// BlockedBead pairs a bead with its blocker classification.
228	type BlockedBead struct {
229		Bead
230		Blocker Blocker `json:"blocker"`
231	}
232	
233	// DepIDs returns a flat list of dependency IDs for this bead.
234	func (b *Bead) DepIDs() []string {
235		var ids []string
236		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
237			ids = append(ids, d.DependsOnID)
238		}
239		return ids
240	}
241	
242	// HasDep returns true if the bead depends on the given ID.
243	func (b *Bead) HasDep(id string) bool {
244		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
245			if d.DependsOnID == id {
246				return true
247			}
248		}
249		return false
250	}
251	
252	// AddDep adds a dependency if it doesn't already exist.
253	func (b *Bead) AddDep(depID, depType string) {
254		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
255			if d.DependsOnID == depID {
256				return // already exists
257			}
258		}
259		b.Dependencies = append(b.Dependencies, Dependency{
260			IssueID:     b.ID,
261			DependsOnID: depID,
262			Type:        depType,
263			CreatedAt:   time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
264		})
265	}
266	
267	// RemoveDep removes a dependency by target ID.
268	func (b *Bead) RemoveDep(depID string) {
269		var filtered []Dependency
270		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
271			if d.DependsOnID != depID {
272				filtered = append(filtered, d)
273			}
274		}
275		b.Dependencies = filtered
276	}
277
bead-record.schema.json

1	{
2	  "$schema": "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema",
3	  "$id": "https://ddx.dev/schemas/bead-record.schema.json",
4	  "title": "DDx Bead Record",
5	  "description": "Base bead-schema record used for DDx bead-backed collections. Unknown fields are allowed for compatibility with bd/br exports and DDx domain-specific extensions.",
6	  "type": "object",
7	  "required": [
8	    "id",
9	    "title",
10	    "status",
11	    "priority",
12	    "issue_type",
13	    "created_at",
14	    "updated_at"
15	  ],
16	  "properties": {
17	    "id": {
18	      "type": "string",
19	      "minLength": 1
20	    },
21	    "title": {
22	      "type": "string",
23	      "minLength": 1
24	    },
25	    "status": {
26	      "type": "string",
27	      "enum": ["open", "in_progress", "closed", "blocked", "proposed", "cancelled"]
28	    },
29	    "priority": {
30	      "type": "integer",
31	      "minimum": 0,
32	      "maximum": 4
33	    },
34	    "issue_type": {
35	      "type": "string",
36	      "minLength": 1
37	    },

39	      "type": "string"
40	    },

42	      "type": "string",
43	      "format": "date-time"
44	    },
45	    "created_by": {
46	      "type": "string"
47	    },
48	    "updated_at": {
49	      "type": "string",
50	      "format": "date-time"
51	    },
52	    "labels": {
53	      "type": "array",
54	      "items": {
55	        "type": "string"
56	      }
57	    },
58	    "parent": {
59	      "type": "string"
60	    },

63	    },

75	        "properties": {

87	          },
88	          "created_at": {
89	            "type": "string",
90	            "format": "date-time"
91	          },
92	          "created_by": {
93	            "type": "string"
94	          },
95	          "metadata": {
96	            "type": "string"
97	          }
98	        },

100	      }
101	    }
102	  },

library/artifacts/operator-prompt/template.md

1	---
2	ddx:
3	  id: OP-NNN
4	  depends_on: []
5	operator_prompt:
6	  schema_version: 1
7	  issue_type: operator-prompt
8	  status: proposed
9	  priority: 2                    # 0..4 (clamped to MinPriority..MaxPriority)
10	  source: web-ui                 # web-ui | cli | api
11	  labels:
12	    - kind:operator-prompt
13	    - source:web-ui
14	---
15	# Operator Prompt: <Short Title>
16	
17	## Prompt body
18	
19	The verbatim instruction the operator submitted. The execute-bead harness
20	treats this body as the contract — there is no separate acceptance criteria
21	verifier for operator-prompt beads. Be specific and complete.
22	
23	## Approval flow
24	
25	Operator-prompt beads are created in `proposed` status and excluded from
26	`ddx work` / execute-loop drain until an operator approves them by
27	transitioning the status to `open`. Allowed transitions out of `proposed`:
28	
29	- `proposed → open` — approve, queue for execution
30	- `proposed → cancelled` — decline, do not run
31	
32	No other transitions out of `proposed` are permitted.
33	
34	## Hard constraint: no self-mutation
35	
36	An operator-prompt bead's execution **may not** create, edit, or close
37	another operator-prompt bead. The constraint is enforced at bead-create
38	time by `OperatorPromptMutationGuard` and cannot be bypassed by labels,
39	metadata, or harness configuration. If a chained workflow is required,
40	file the follow-up work as a regular `task` bead.
41	
42	## Acceptance
43	
44	The auto-AC stub for operator-prompt beads is:
45	
46	> Agent must produce a diff or no_changes rationale; the prompt body is the
47	> contract.
48	
49	Override the stub only when you want a structural AC verifier to run
50	(rare — the prompt body is normally the only contract).
51
